author_line: Birkhoff 1911 ("General theory of linear difference equations")
rank_class_addressed: regular and integer-rank irregular (single-slope Newton polygon)
gap_addressed: provides formal-series existence in the integer-rank case; defines the Newton-polygon stratification at infinity for linear difference equations
gap_still_open_after: fractional-rank case (slopes p/q with q>1); analytic / sectorial existence of solutions matching the formal series
siarc_use: cited by umbrella v2.0 main.tex line 295 (key Birkhoff1930 — likely typo for the 1911 paper, see unexpected_finds.json discrepancy D-01); foundational for any B4 statement at all d

author_line: Adams (C. R.) 1928 ("On the irregular cases of the linear ordinary difference equation")
rank_class_addressed: second-order irregular linear difference equations (parallel to Adams's ODE work)
gap_addressed: establishes the method B–T 1933 generalises to arbitrary order n; gives a complete formal-series treatment of the n=2 case
gap_still_open_after: extension to arbitrary order n; fractional-rank
siarc_use: pre-cited by B–T 1933; should be cited in any rigorous Phase-2 attempt at d=3 because the SIARC PCF stratum is order-2

author_line: Birkhoff & Trjitzinsky 1933 ("Analytic Theory of Singular Difference Equations")
rank_class_addressed: all ranks including fractional, formal level only
rigour_tier: theorem (formal); heuristic for sectorial analytic continuation
gap_addressed: formal-series existence Thm 1; formal classification by Stokes data Thm 2; covers fractional ranks left open by Birkhoff 1911
gap_still_open_after: Borel summability; sectorial analytic existence; Stokes-multiplier rigidity (none of these in B–T 1933)
siarc_use: cited as the foundational reference for SIARC B4; should be re-cited with explicit caveat that B–T 1933 establishes formal but not analytic content

author_line: Turrittin 1955 ("Convergent solutions of ordinary linear homogeneous difference equations")
gap_addressed: establishes convergent (not merely formal) sectorial solutions in the regular-singular case; first rigorous formal-to-analytic upgrade for difference equations
gap_still_open_after: irregular cases (slopes > 0 with divergent formal series); these need Borel summation / multisummability
siarc_use: directly relevant to upper bound psi_upper(d) in Q3; should be cited in PCF-2 v1.3 §B4 discussion as the rigorous version of "convergent" for the regular-singular case

author_line: Wasow 1965 ("Asymptotic Expansions for Ordinary Differential Equations")
rank_class_addressed: regular and irregular linear difference equations; canonical textbook restatement of B–T 1933 in modern notation
gap_addressed: provides the modern reformulation of B–T 1933 in the language of WKB and Birkhoff matrices; refines the polynomial-coefficient sub-case
siarc_use: canonical secondary citation for any SIARC paper that names "Birkhoff–Trjitzinsky theory"; the polynomial-coefficient refinement (§X.3) is what underwrites psi_upper(d) <= 2d

author_line: Immink 1984/1991+ ("Asymptotics of Analytic Difference Equations" and Compositio Math papers)
venue: Lecture Notes in Mathematics 1085, Springer-Verlag; Compositio Mathematica
rank_class_addressed: rank-1 irregular difference equations; establishes Borel-1 summability of the formal series
gap_addressed: first rigorous Borel-summability theorem for irregular linear difference equations; sectorial-asymptotic existence for the divergent B–T formal series in the rank-1 case
gap_still_open_after: rank > 1 / fractional-rank multisummability (handed to Braaksma); resurgence-theoretic sharpening
siarc_use: directly underwrites the formal-to-analytic upgrade required by SIARC B4 at d ≥ 3 in the regular-singular Newton-polygon case; should be cited in any Phase-2 proof attempt

author_line: Braaksma 1991/1992 ("Multisummability of formal power series solutions...")
venue: J. Differential Equations 92, 45–75 (1991) AND Annales de l'Institut Fourier 42, 517–540 (1992) [exact venue depends on the version cited; multiple closely related papers]
rank_class_addressed: multisummable / fractional-rank cases (slopes p/q with q>1)
gap_addressed: establishes multisummability of formal series solutions of meromorphic difference and differential equations in the multi-slope case; closes the analytic gap in B–T 1933's fractional-rank theorem
gap_still_open_after: resurgence-theoretic alien-derivative content (handed to Costin / Écalle / Sauzin)
siarc_use: relevant only at SIARC arithmetic loci where Newton polygon degenerates and a fractional-rank slope appears; not used in the bulk of B4

author_line: Costin 1998+ ("On Borel summation and Stokes phenomena for rank-1 nonlinear systems...") and "Asymptotics and Borel Summability" (CRC Press, 2008)
venue: Duke Mathematical Journal 93, 289–344 (1998); CRC Press monograph (2008)
gap_addressed: provides the rigorous resurgence framework that pins half-Stokes ambiguity via median summation; underwrites the H4 / channel-theory v1.3 §3.5 median prescription
gap_still_open_after: non-rank-1 cases need Loday-Richaud / Sauzin extension
siarc_use: cited by CT v1.3 as the foundation of op:cc-median-resurgence-execute; orthogonal to the bulk of T1 but relevant to op:cc-formal-borel reframe

author_line: Loday-Richaud 2016 ("Divergent Series, Summability and Resurgence II: Simple and Multiple Summability")
rank_class_addressed: complete textbook synthesis of multisummability + resurgence for ODE and difference equations
gap_addressed: gives the modern unified treatment of Braaksma + Costin + Écalle in one volume; explicit chapter on difference equations
gap_still_open_after: open research-level content (alien calculus higher orders) not in the textbook
siarc_use: canonical modern reference for any SIARC paper that names "multisummability" or "resurgence"; should replace ad-hoc citations to multiple Braaksma / Costin / Écalle papers
